Category

Containers & Planters

Good container choices reduce stress on both plants and growers. These guides cover root volume, drainage, material tradeoffs, and how planter choices affect watering frequency and crop performance.

Primary topic hub

Containers & Planters

Container growing depends on physical structure more than most beginners expect. This hub organizes container guidance around root space, drainage, crop fit, and the decisions that make container gardens easier to maintain.

Open topic hub Browse archive
Topic pathways

Structured routes from the primary hub

Use these grouped sections to move from beginner questions into setup choices, troubleshooting, and deeper follow-up reads.

Topic pathway

Beginner guides

Begin with realistic container sizing and the crops that fit compact planters well.

Containers February 4, 2026

Container Depth for Tomatoes, Peppers, and Herbs: How Much Root Space Matters

Container size is one of the highest-leverage decisions in small-space gardening. When root volume is too small, every other care task becomes harder.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Urban Gardening March 10, 2026

Balcony Herb Garden for Beginners: A Simple Container Setup That Actually Works

A practical beginner plan for turning a balcony into a manageable herb setup that stays readable, productive, and realistic to maintain.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Topic pathway

Setup guides

These guides connect container choices to medium structure, balcony crop plans, and everyday upkeep.

Soil & Fertilizers February 26, 2026

Potting Mix vs. Raised Bed Soil for Containers: What Actually Belongs in Pots

Container plants need air space, drainage, and predictable structure. That is why raised bed soil and in-ground blends usually disappoint in pots.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Urban Gardening January 28, 2026

Best Vegetables for Small Balconies: What Produces Well in Tight Spaces

Not every vegetable belongs on a balcony. These are the crops most likely to reward limited square footage without turning watering and support into a daily headache.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Topic pathway

Troubleshooting

Use these pieces when containers are drying unevenly, roots are stressed, or plant vigor starts slipping.

Plant Problems March 5, 2026

Why Are Basil Leaves Turning Yellow Indoors?

Yellow basil rarely has a single universal cause. The pattern of yellowing, the growing medium, and the recent care routine usually tell the story.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Hydroponics March 6, 2026

Hydroponic Lettuce Growing Slowly? How to Diagnose the Real Bottleneck

Slow hydroponic lettuce is usually a systems problem, not a mystery deficiency. The goal is to find the bottleneck without overcorrecting five variables at once.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Topic pathway

Advanced topics

These guides help bridge container growing with indoor support systems and tighter environmental control.

Grow Lights March 7, 2026

Best Grow Lights for Herbs: What Actually Matters Before You Buy

The best grow light for herbs is the one that matches your footprint, crop density, and tolerance for heat and visual clutter. This guide focuses on fit before hype.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Grow Lights February 22, 2026

How Many Hours Should Grow Lights Run for Herbs?

A longer light schedule is not always a better one. Herbs need enough daily light, but they also benefit from a stable dark period and realistic fixture placement.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
